name: Salesforce
description: A cloud-based CRM platform providing APIs (REST, SOAP, GraphQL) to programmatically access and manage CRM data, metadata, and platform features.

auth_methods.note: Salesforce primarily uses OAuth 2.0 flows (Web Server, User-Agent, Username-Password, JWT Bearer, Refresh Token, Device Flow). Access tokens (Bearer) and session IDs (token) are supported. Salesforce does not use a simple API key mechanic.

credential_access.note: Developers can sign up for a free Developer Edition org which includes API access and lets them create Connected Apps to obtain client_id and client_secret for OAuth.

api_surface.note: Salesforce exposes a broad REST API (sObjects, query, composite, tooling, metadata) and also provides a GraphQL API for querying data; authentication via OAuth tokens applies to both.

buildability.note: With a free Developer Edition and ability to create Connected Apps (client_id/secret) plus documented OAuth flows and APIs, integrations can be built today without manual sales approval. Some production orgs/editions may restrict API access and require upgrading/contacting sales, but development is feasible via Developer Edition.

queries: Create Connected App Salesforce consumer key secret
Salesforce Developer Edition signup API access
Salesforce REST API overview sObjects coverage
Which Editions Include API Access Salesforce

name: HubSpot
description: A CRM platform offering REST APIs and developer tools to integrate with HubSpot CRM objects, marketing, CMS, and other product areas.

auth_methods.note: Official docs state OAuth 2.0 for public apps and Private Apps (account-scoped bearer tokens). API keys are deprecated (removed). 'token' denotes Private App bearer tokens. 'other' included to reflect deprecated API keys which the docs explicitly note were removed (legacy). 'none_public' included not as a supported method but to acknowledge no unauthenticated public API access.

credential_access.note: Private apps can be created in the HubSpot UI to generate scoped access tokens; developer docs show steps to create an app and obtain client ID/secret and to create private apps in account settings without human approval or paid plan. Developer account app creation page documents Client ID/secret issuance.

api_surface.note: HubSpot provides a broad REST API surface covering CRM objects (contacts, companies, deals), marketing, CMS, and more; docs root and API reference show base URL and many endpoints. There is no indication that GraphQL or SDK-only access replaces REST in the official reference.

buildability.note: Documentation and self-service credential issuance (OAuth client creation in developer account and Private App token generation) exist, enabling building integrations today without manual approvals. Publishing to Marketplace may require partner program steps, but building integrations for an account is immediate.

queries: HubSpot authentication overview OAuth private apps
HubSpot private apps create token generate
HubSpot API reference overview REST API base URL
HubSpot create an app client ID client secret

name: Pipedrive
description: A sales CRM platform for managing leads, deals, activities and sales pipelines, with a public REST API to integrate and automate workflows.

auth_methods.note: Official API docs enumerate API token (personal API key) and OAuth2 as supported authentication methods.

credential_access.note: Personal API token can be obtained from a user's Settings > Personal preferences > API without contacting support. Documentation shows how to retrieve the token from the account UI, indicating developers can self-serve credentials. Marketplace/app publication has review, but basic API access uses account token.

api_surface.note: Pipedrive provides a comprehensive REST API (v1) covering core product objects (deals, persons, organizations, activities, products, pipelines, etc.) as documented in the API reference root.

buildability.note: Public REST API docs exist and developers can obtain a personal API token from their account without human approval, so integrations can be built today. Publishing apps to the Marketplace requires app submission/review but that doesn't block initial development.

queries: Pipedrive API authentication api token oauth2 developers.pipedrive.com
How to find your API token Pipedrive support
Pipedrive developers apps register client id client secret

pass1.reasoning: The homepage and API documentation confirm Pipedrive is a sales CRM platform for managing leads, deals, activities and sales pipelines with a public REST API. The description accurately reflects the product.

The OAuth 2.0 documentation page (https://developers.pipedrive.com/docs/api/v1/Oauth) confirms OAuth 2.0 is supported. The API reference confirms API token authentication is supported. Both authentication methods are officially documented.

The cited evidence_url (https://support.pipedrive.com/en/article/how-to-find-your-api-token) returns a 404 error. However, the correct support article at https://support.pipedrive.com/en/article/how-can-i-find-my-personal-api-key confirms the value is correct: 'If you ever need your personal API key, it can be found under Settings > Personal preferences > API.' The value itself is accurate but the evidence URL is broken.

The API reference at https://developers.pipedrive.com/docs/api/v1/ confirms it is a comprehensive REST API (v1) with broad coverage including endpoints for deals, persons, organizations, activities, products, pipelines, and many other core objects.

The record correctly states developers can build today. Public REST API docs exist and developers can obtain personal API tokens from their account settings without human approval. The app approval process page confirms that marketplace submission requires review, but this doesn't block initial development or private app creation.

name: Attio
description: A modern CRM and workspace for managing relationships, contacts, and company data with a programmable API.

auth_methods.note: The official Authentication reference page (meta/description) states Attio uses API keys and OAuth 2.0 for authentication; SDK examples (npm/pypi) also show api_key usage.

credential_access.note: Pricing page advertises 'Start for free'. The Get Started docs (https://docs.attio.com/get-started) describe signup, dashboard onboarding and how to access API credentials, indicating developers can obtain credentials themselves after signup.

api_surface.note: The API reference overview page (meta/description) mentions 'endpoints, models, REST examples' and there are reference pages for product objects (e.g. Collections, People) showing multiple endpoints.

buildability.note: Public API docs exist and developers can obtain API keys via signup (free tier available). You can sign up for an account and generate API keys to start building.

name: Twenty
description: Open-source CRM built with Next.js and Supabase; intended to be self-hosted or deployed by users rather than a vendor-hosted public API.

auth_methods.note: Auth for the application is implemented via Supabase (project README references SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY). There is no evidence of a vendor-hosted public API supporting OAuth2/API keys/basic/token/JWT on twenty.com; app-level auth relies on Supabase tokens for self-hosted instances (classified as 'other').

credential_access.note: Repository README and docs show instructions and environment variables for obtaining and using Supabase credentials to run the app locally or self-host; developers can get Supabase free-tier credentials without vendor approval to run Twenty.

api_surface.note: There is no vendor-hosted public REST/GraphQL API documented on twenty.com. The codebase exposes Next.js API routes and integrates with Supabase for DB access, but these are part of the self-hosted app (not a documented public API).

buildability.note: You can build integrations by self-hosting Twenty and using its internal API routes or the Supabase backend (requires signing up for Supabase to obtain keys). There is no hosted vendor API to directly integrate with without self-hosting. (Normalized main_blocker from 'no_vendor_hosted_api' to allowed enum 'no_public_api'.)

queries: Twenty CRM API developer documentation site:twenty.com "API" OR "developer" OR "docs"

name: Podio
description: A collaborative work platform for managing projects, CRM, and workflows with a RESTful API to automate and integrate Podio data and actions.

auth_methods.note: Documentation states Podio uses OAuth 2 (authorization code flow), supports client credentials (machine-to-machine) and accepts Bearer tokens; Podio does not use API keys for general access. Mapped 'client_credentials' to 'oauth2' and 'bearer_token' to 'token' to match allowed enum values.

credential_access.note: Developers can register an application in the developer console to obtain client_id and client_secret instantly after logging in; no mention of paid-only or partner gating.

api_surface.note: API docs describe a RESTful HTTP API covering many resource types (items, apps, tasks, users, orgs, spaces, contacts, etc.), with CRUD operations.

buildability.note: Public REST API docs plus self-serve client credentials are available; developers can register apps and use OAuth flows to obtain tokens and call endpoints.

Podio API docs "Podio API" documentation developer site:podio.com OR site:developers.podio.com OR site:help.podio.com OR site:podio.developerhub.io
Podio API pricing "API" "pricing" site:podio.com OR site:developers.podio.com OR site:help.podio.com

name: Zoho CRM
description: A cloud-based customer relationship management platform providing sales, marketing, and support automation with extensive REST APIs and SDKs.

auth_methods.note: Zoho CRM uses OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code, self-client, refresh token); API requests use Bearer tokens. No public GraphQL or basic auth documented.

credential_access.note: Developers can sign in to the Zoho API Console to create OAuth clients or self-clients and generate client ID/secret and grant tokens without needing partner approval. API usage is subject to edition limits; additional units can be purchased.

api_surface.note: Zoho CRM exposes a broad REST API (v2/v8), covering modules like Leads, Contacts, Accounts, Deals, Products, Quotes, Bulk APIs, search, fields, layouts, attachments, etc.

buildability.note: Public REST APIs and OAuth credentials can be obtained via the API Console; documentation and SDKs are available, so integrations can be built today. API rate limits depend on CRM edition and may require purchasing additional API units for high-volume usage.

queries: Zoho CRM API documentation developer site:zoho.com/crm/developer/docs
Zoho CRM API pricing site:zoho.com "Zoho CRM" API pricing limits "API"

name: Close
description: Close is a CRM focused on sales teams that provides a REST API to access and manage leads, activities, contacts, and related CRM objects.

auth_methods.note: Close uses API Keys; keys are supplied via HTTP Basic auth (API key as username, blank password) or an Authorization header with Basic <base64(API_KEY:)>.

credential_access.note: API keys are created by a logged-in user in Settings -> API Keys in the Close dashboard; documentation shows a user can create keys from the UI. Docs note that if you don't see API Keys, organization permissions may restrict visibility (admin settings), but generally keys are self-service from the dashboard.

api_surface.note: The developer docs state the Close API is a REST API with a base URL and example endpoints (e.g., /api/v1/lead/). Official docs include API reference and SDKs for multiple languages, indicating broad coverage of product objects.

buildability.note: Public REST docs exist and API keys can be created self-serve in the dashboard, so a developer can build integrations today. Only potential blocker is organization-level permission settings may prevent some users from creating keys (requires admin in that org).

queries: Close API authentication API keys basic auth developer docs
How do I create an API Key Close docs

name: Copper
description: A CRM built for Google Workspace users that manages contacts, pipelines, and sales workflows.

auth_methods.note: Developer docs explicitly list OAuth2.0 and API Keys (token-based) as the two supported authentication methods.

credential_access.note: The OAuth quickstart shows developers register an app in the Copper developer dashboard to obtain client_id and client_secret (self-serve). The public pricing page indicates Copper offers free trials / 'Start free, no credit card required' (https://www.copper.com/pricing), but no official page explicitly states API access is available on the free plan, so classification is medium confidence.

api_surface.note: The developer site states the Copper Developer API provides a RESTful interface with JSON-formatted responses and 'access most Copper resources.'

buildability.note: Public developer docs (developer site, authentication, and OAuth quickstart) provide REST API reference and instructions to register apps and obtain credentials, enabling development today without manual approval. The only remaining uncertainty is whether specific plan limits apply (pricing page does not explicitly gate API access).

name: DealCloud
description: DealCloud (by Intapp) is a deal and relationship management platform for investment banking, private equity, and corporate development, providing CRM, pipeline, and workflow APIs for integrations.

auth_methods.note: Official docs list OAuth2 (Authorization Code and Client Credentials), API Key via X-Api-Key header, and Personal Access Tokens (used as Bearer tokens).

credential_access.note: Docs state API Keys and Personal Access Tokens are provisioned by your DealCloud administrator or by contacting DealCloud support; non-admins must contact support/sales.

api_surface.note: Swagger JSON at /swagger/v1/swagger.json shows a REST API (paths like /contacts, /accounts, /oauth/token) and securityDefinitions; the Swagger UI is hosted at the docs root. The API covers core objects (Contacts, Accounts, Operations) indicating broad coverage.

buildability.note: Public docs exist and the REST API is documented, but API credentials must be provisioned by an admin or support/sales contact, preventing immediate self-serve developer access; requires contacting admin or sales.

name: Zendesk
description: Customer service platform providing ticketing, user and organization management, messaging, and related support APIs.

auth_methods.note: Official developer docs list OAuth 2.0, API token (used like an API key), and basic auth (email/password or email/token). Support article details API token usage and basic auth variant.

credential_access.note: Generating an API token requires an admin to create it in Admin Center; tokens are not obtainable by a developer without admin privileges. OAuth app creation and admin-level settings likewise require account/admin setup.

api_surface.note: The Ticketing API reference is a broad REST API covering tickets, users, organizations, ticket fields and many core product objects. Developer portal lists multiple APIs and SDKs, indicating wide coverage.

buildability.note: Technically well-documented and usable, but API credentials (API tokens) must be generated by an account administrator, preventing a third-party developer from self-serving credentials on an arbitrary Zendesk instance.

queries: Zendesk API authentication oauth2 api token basic
Zendesk generate API token admin center
Zendesk API rate limits vary by plan

name: Intercom
description: Customer messaging, helpdesk, and product support platform (inbox, conversations, users, and automation).

auth_methods.note: Intercom supports OAuth 2.0 for public apps and Personal Access Tokens (Bearer tokens) for server-to-server/private apps; requests use Authorization: Bearer <token>.

credential_access.note: You can create an app in the Developer Hub and receive credentials (Access Token for private apps or client ID/secret for public OAuth apps) immediately from your workspace.

api_surface.note: Intercom exposes a broad REST API (users, leads, conversations, companies, events, tags, etc.) and also provides a GraphQL endpoint (https://api.intercom.io/graphql) as noted in the API reference.

buildability.note: Public docs plus self-serve credential creation in the Developer Hub allow building integrations today. (Enterprise-only product features may still require paid plans or Sales contact, but API credentials can be obtained from a workspace.)

name: Freshdesk
description: Customer support ticketing and helpdesk platform with APIs to manage tickets, contacts, agents, solutions and more.

auth_methods.note: Official docs state API key usage via HTTP Basic Auth (API key as username, 'X' as password) and also mention OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens.

credential_access.note: Support article shows an individual user can obtain their API key from their profile settings after logging in; implies self-serve access. Freshdesk offers a free plan and trial signup (pricing page) so developers can create accounts without manual approval.

api_surface.note: API v2 is a REST API covering tickets, contacts, agents, companies, conversations, solutions, forums, time entries and more per the official API reference.

buildability.note: Public REST API docs and self-serve API key (via profile) exist; developers can sign up on Free plan or trial and immediately obtain API credentials to build integrations.

credential_access.note: Help Center shows developers can create personal API tokens from Settings > Company > Developers (no human approval or paid plan mentioned). name: Pylon
description: A platform for customer support and helpdesk workflows that provides a RESTful developer API for interacting with account and product data.

auth_methods.note: Authentication uses Bearer tokens provided in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <token>). The docs state admin users create API tokens in the Pylon dashboard.

credential_access.note: Docs explicitly state API tokens are created by Admin users in the Pylon dashboard, implying credentials must be created by an admin rather than self-serve by any end-user.

api_surface.note: Official docs are a REST API (endpoints like GET /api/v1/me referenced). The docs are the API root. The breadth (whether most product objects are covered) is not fully documented on a single page; the API reference exists but the fetch did not enumerate all endpoints to classify broad vs narrow.

buildability.note: Although the API is documented and uses bearer tokens, API tokens must be generated by Admin users in the dashboard, so a developer without admin access cannot self-serve obtain working credentials. This blocks building immediately for a typical external developer account.

name: Slack
description: Team communication platform providing messaging, channels, and integrations via HTTP Web APIs, Events (webhooks), and real-time APIs.

auth_methods.note: Slack recommends OAuth 2.0 for app installation and issues bearer tokens (bot/user/app tokens) used in the Authorization: Bearer <token> header; tokens are the primary authentication mechanism for Web API calls.

credential_access.note: Developers can create a Slack App in the App Dashboard to obtain client ID/secret and install to a workspace to receive tokens; this can be done on a workspace (including free plan) without human approval.

api_surface.note: Slack exposes a broad Web API (HTTP REST methods) plus Events (webhook delivery), RTM (WebSocket) and additional APIs like SCIM and Admin; the docs root lists Web API, Events API, RTM, SCIM, Admin, and SDKs.

buildability.note: Public docs and self-serve credential creation exist, so you can build integrations immediately. Note some admin/provisioning endpoints (e.g., SCIM, Admin APIs) are restricted to Enterprise Grid or require paid/enterprise plans.

name: Twilio
description: Cloud communications platform providing REST APIs and SDKs for SMS, Voice, Video, WhatsApp, and other messaging/real-time communications services.

auth_methods.note: Twilio's main REST API uses HTTP Basic Auth with Account SID as username and Auth Token as password. API Key SID/Secret can also be used in place of Account SID/Auth Token (used in HTTP Basic Auth).

credential_access.note: Developers can sign up for a free Twilio trial account and receive Account SID and Auth Token (and create API Keys) without human approval; trial has usage limits.

api_surface.note: Twilio provides REST APIs across its products plus official helper libraries (SDKs) in multiple languages; the docs hub lists REST APIs, SDKs, quickstarts, and API references covering messaging, voice, video, etc.

buildability.note: Public REST APIs and self-serve credentials are available; developers can build integrations immediately. Some specific product features (e.g., WhatsApp Business onboarding, short codes) require additional approvals or provisioning which may require applications/fees (see evidence).

credential_access.note: Developers can sign in to the Zoho API Console to create a client and obtain client_id and client_secret without mention of payment or manual approval.

api_surface.note: The REST API v2 provides endpoints for messages, channels, users, bots, webhooks and more (full reference available in the v2 docs).

name: Pumble
description: Team chat and collaboration platform with a REST API add-on for automating workspace actions (messages, channels, members, webhooks).

auth_methods.note: Pumble uses API keys sent in the X-Api-Key header to authenticate REST API requests; API keys are created in the workspace API section.

credential_access.note: The docs state the API add-on can be installed in a workspace and API keys generated via the workspace UI. The public pricing page does not indicate API access requires paid plan or sales/partnership approval.

api_surface.note: Documentation describes a REST API (Swagger UI at /api/docs) and lists ability to create messages, manage channels, members, webhooks and more; full endpoint docs exposed via Swagger on workspace.

buildability.note: Developer can install the API addon in a workspace and generate API keys via the UI (self-serve). No evidence that admin/partner approval or paid-only access is required, so building is feasible after routine key signup in a workspace.

name: Discord
description: A real-time chat platform for communities and developers offering text/voice/video, servers (guilds), channels, and a programmable public API for bots and integrations.

auth_methods.note: Discord supports OAuth2 flows (authorization code, PKCE, client credentials, etc.). OAuth token exchanges use HTTP Basic auth (client_id:client_secret base64) for some flows, and bots authenticate to the REST API using a Bot token sent in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bot <token>). Evidence: OAuth2 docs and Applications dashboard docs.

credential_access.note: Developers can create applications and add bot users in the Developer Portal to obtain Client ID, Client Secret, and Bot Token without human approval or paid plans.

api_surface.note: Discord provides a broad REST API covering channels, guilds, users, messages, etc., plus a real-time Gateway (WebSocket) for events (both documented in the Developer Docs).

buildability.note: Public docs and self-serve credential creation exist, allowing developers to build today. Operational considerations: bots above certain scale or accessing privileged data (e.g., GUILD_MEMBERS, PRESENCE) require intent enablement and verification (human review) as described in the verification and privileged intents docs, but these do not prevent initial development.

queries: Discord developer applications create app bot token client secret

name: Telegram
description: Instant messaging platform providing user/client APIs (MTProto/TDLib) and a HTTP-based Bot API for bots and integrations.

auth_methods.note: The Bot API authenticates using a bot token issued by BotFather (token). The Telegram API (MTProto) requires an api_id and api_hash obtained via my.telegram.org (API key/secret). Both are documented on the official Telegram developer site.

credential_access.note: Developers can create an application at my.telegram.org to receive api_id and api_hash, and can create bots via BotFather to receive bot tokens, without human approval or paid partnership.

api_surface.note: Telegram provides a RESTful HTTP Bot API for bots and an MTProto API (used by client libraries and TDLib). The docs site lists Bot API, Telegram API (MTProto), and TDLib, indicating broad coverage of product objects via these APIs.

buildability.note: Public documentation exists and credentials (bot token or api_id/api_hash) can be obtained via BotFather and my.telegram.org respectively, enabling development today.

queries: Telegram developer documentation core.telegram.org developer docs root
site:core.telegram.org obtaining_api_id OR "obtain an API identifier"
Telegram BotFather create bot token Bot API how to create bot site:core.telegram.org "BotFather"

name: WhatsApp Business
description: WhatsApp Business Platform lets businesses send and receive messages at scale via Meta-hosted Cloud API or a self‑hosted on‑premises API, with support for message templates, media, and webhooks.

auth_methods.note: Authentication uses OAuth-style access tokens (Bearer tokens) including short-lived and long-lived tokens obtained via the Meta App dashboard and Facebook Login flows; API calls use Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>.

credential_access.note: Developers can create a Meta App and generate temporary access tokens and test phone numbers from the dashboard for free testing. Production usage requires business verification and a WABA but initial credentials for development are self-serve and free.

api_surface.note: The WhatsApp Business Platform provides REST endpoints (Cloud API and on‑premises) covering messaging, media, templates, webhooks and account management; SDK client libraries are referenced from the docs.

buildability.note: You can obtain test tokens and a test phone number immediately via the Meta for Developers dashboard and call REST endpoints (Cloud API) for development. Production requires business verification and phone number approval but does not block initial development.

queries: WhatsApp Business Platform authentication access tokens bearer
WhatsApp Cloud API get started generate temporary access token test phone number
WhatsApp Business API pricing billing production charges
WhatsApp Business on-premises API reference endpoints
WhatsApp Business Solution Provider BSP partner directory

name: Aircall
description: Cloud phone system & call center platform — REST API for calls, contacts, and webhooks to build integrations.

auth_methods.note: Aircall docs reference OAuth2 and personal access tokens / API tokens in places.

credential_access.note: Developers can register apps and obtain credentials from the developer portal after signing up.

api_surface.note: Site exposes an OpenAPI JSON at /openapi.json.

buildability.note: Public REST API available; requires account signup and app credential creation.

name: Vonage
description: APIs and SDKs for voice, SMS, messaging, video and related cloud communications services.

auth_methods.note: Official docs describe API key & secret usage, Application JWTs for apps, and other signing/auth concepts (signatures).

credential_access.note: Developer documentation and the dashboard sign-up show that developers can create an account and obtain API key & secret from the Developer Dashboard without manual approval.

api_surface.note: Vonage exposes RESTful APIs across its product surfaces (SMS, Voice, Messages, Verify, etc.) and provides official SDKs that wrap those REST APIs; documentation root lists product APIs and SDKs.

buildability.note: Public docs and self-serve account signup + API key issuance allow developers to start building immediately. Some specific channels/features (e.g., WhatsApp Business) require additional verification/onboarding documented separately and may block that channel until approved.

queries: Vonage API key secret Developer Dashboard
Vonage developer sign up get api key
